paragraphs.body: Live With Purpose

annotations.comment: Natalie, this is WAY over the word limit — 25% longer than requested. Please do try to respect my and your classmates’ time and stick to the word count.

paragraphs.body: Ugh, my chest hurts and I'm running out of breath. I haven't done this much work since high school football. Shit, I feel him catching up. Like that instinctual feeling you get when you know someone’s staring at you from across a room. Like I can sense his aura growing and spreading into my own.
"You cannot run forever, little one. This fault is none but your own, you knew what shall become of you, as warned."

annotations.comment: This should be indented. Who is speaking?

paragraphs.body: He's right, but I couldn’t find myself to believe it. Ever since I started having those dreams 3 weeks ago, I knew something felt off but there was no way I could accept it. Nightly premonitions, warning me that if I failed to return the stone, consequence would follow.

annotations.comment: Phrasing is off — bring myself to believe it?

paragraphs.body: After an unforgettable vacation in Chile, I had to return for my final year of undergrad and prep for medical school. I studied my ass off for the last few years, did well on the MCAT, sent in all my applications, and was getting ready for interviews. This vacation was a gift from my parents for all the hard work I've been putting in. We went into the mountains the day before we left to return home, my first dream occurring later that night. I never would've thought anything of it, just my own brain's anxiety making up an intriguing back-story for this awesome clump of orange quartz I found in one of the mini caves on our excursion. Unfortunately, I'm a little more concerned now, as it is glowing and pulsating.

annotations.text: for the last few years, did well on the MCAT, sent in all my applications, and was getting ready for interviews
annotations.comment: Do we need to know all of this?

annotations.text: never would've thought anything of it, just my own brain's anxiety making up an intriguing back-story for this awesome clump of orange quartz I found in one of the mini caves on our excursion
annotations.comment: This made me laugh, in a good way. I’m confused — is he concerned more about the pulsing orange quartz, or about the voice speaking to him, or the being chasing him?

paragraphs.body: I dodged and I weaved, parkouring through the woods near my campus house at 3AM, bouncing off logs and rocks, hopping over little streams and shrubs. He seemed to outperform me in a straight line, but thankfully he isn't fully spectral, and is unable to pass through material objects. But then, how did he end up in my room in the first place?
"You must be punished, just as a thief is shown no remorse, and a low born bears no excuse."

Low born? Is that racist? Just then it occurred to me that this is indeed the same individual who has been present in my dreams. An ancient figure, dressed in faded textile-like drapes with half of his large chest sticking out and man-made sandals. He was also decorated in large, hanging feathered earrings. Was this an ancient figure originating from the very mountains I visited? Just as I was trying to remember what the tour guide told us about the historic residents of the area, I was pleasantly startled by what was uttered next by this spectral being.

annotations.comment: Okay, I genuinely LOL’d at this.
I think it would make more sense if he got a good luck at the figure here — surely he would have recognized him before now?

paragraphs.body: "Enough running...please.."
Please? Why did he just say please. At first he was so stern and vengeant. Now he's pleading? I looked down at the stone and uttered to myself, "what are you?"

annotations.comment: I find it funny that the guy doesn’t want to run! I do think that the narrator talking to the stone is confusing. Did he start having the dreams the night after he brought the stone back o his hotel?

paragraphs.body: At that instant, everything froze, but I still heard leaves, branches, and shrubs crunching as slow footsteps approached me from behind.
"I told you boy, you were forewarned. So which hand will it be? Never mind that, I shall have both."

As he swung down on my extended left arm from mid stride, a giant roar let out from the street above the woods. A garbage truck. Wow, is it 6AM already? This moment of distraction led the ancient one to just miss my wrist and strike the stone, which powerfully kicked him back. The stone now had a giant crack and started glowing and pulsating more aggressively. He then screamed, almost in agony:
"Nooo!"
I unfroze but fell to the dirt, starstruck by what was happening in my own 2 hands. Then, in a flash, there was nothing.

When I woke up my eyes were still closed. I smelled something faintly familiar, like fresh pine and damp Earth, like recent rain fall. I felt sweat quickly accumulating around my hands, feet, and back. I finally opened my eyes. It looked like I was in a tent, a little sliver of daylight creeping inside from about 10 feet in front of me. I stood and walked out to see where I was. I looked around the bright green mountain scape that lay before me.
"What the fu-" *A large bird caws*

Seemingly I was on a great mountain full of lush greenery, surrounded by even larger ones. More astonished by my immediate surroundings, it took me a minute to look down upon the town that was before me. Nearly identical stone buildings, huts even, made up a simple layout of paths and sidewalks, filled with people walking, children running, and merchant carts loudly pronouncing their products for sale. I suddenly jumped and was startled by the footsteps behind me. It was him. It all came back to me now. He said something softly to me, but it sounded like total gibberish, I was so confused I couldn't even hide my expression. He then pulled out a small stone, resembling the one he broke back in the woods. He looked down and it flashed a small glow.

annotations.text: with people walking, children running, and merchant carts loudly pronouncing their produc
annotations.comment: Is this where he was in Chile?

paragraphs.body: "Welcome Boy," he uttered.

annotations.comment: All of these paragraph should be indented. I fixed it for you this time, please fix throughout.

paragraphs.body: "Hello…" I said hesitantly, "here to finally finish the job?" I couldn't hide my sarcasm nor could accept what was going on. I was just so pissed about having to run for my life for hours on end.
"This is my home, where I belong, and you certainly do not." He then sighed and whispered to himself, "Did you truly choose one so bereft of light? A common thief?"
"I didn’t steal anything, it’s just a stupid rock."
He scowled at me," Your ignorance knows no bounds, enough of you. You will labor under me until I know what to do with you."

Before I could finish saying whatever jerky thing that was exiting my mouth next, he flashed a sophisticated dagger, seemingly made of obsidian. Strange since I wouldn’t expect something so well-crafted from such an ancient-being. I played enough videogames and watched enough anime to appreciate the work, but also the threat that it posed.

annotations.comment: I did wonder why he didn’t pull the knife earlier. What happened to the exhaustion from the running? Is this dude still tired out?

paragraphs.body: "Follow," he said sternly.

We went down a path behind the tent I woke up in, opposite of the busy town I first veered at. While walking, I examined the tent more closely and could also tell this was the work of some skillful hands. I then looked ahead and suddenly the mountain scape opened well and wide, revealing a magnificently large plot of farmland ahead. We went down to a small barn where he went in and right back out, tossing me a pile of cloth:
"If you are to be here, at least look to belong."

I unfolded the pile of shit in my arms, and it revealed to look like what he was wearing. God this is ass but whatever. As I took off my dirty bedtime sweatpants and long sleeve shirt, he also tossed me some terribly thin and ugly sandals. I sighed but cooperated while glaring at the dagger hanging by a thread on his side.

annotations.text: God this is ass but whatever.
annotations.context_right: As I took off my dir

paragraphs.body: Weeks passed in a blur, though time seemed to flow differently here. To keep my sanity, I carved a makeshift calendar into the wall of my small sleeping barn. The work was grueling at first, cutting down trees for farmland, hoeing the Earth, and hauling heavy carts. As the days went on, I grew stronger, both physically and mentally. Somewhere between the soreness, sweat, and blisters, I found an unexpected sense of peace, and a deeper appreciation for nature and the Universe. The man and I always shared meals and over time we settled into an unspoken rhythm, cooking together over an indoor firepit and using terribly uneven ceramics, that somehow got the job done. How did I end up here and why? She always used to say everything happens for a reason.

annotations.text: Weeks passed in a blur, though
annotations.comment: So, generally, when you include a huge time jump like this, it’s a sign that the piece doesn’t quite work as flash fiction. It generally (not always) stays in a shorter time frame.

paragraphs.body: After about 3 months I began getting quite agitated. When do I get to go home? After a particularly difficult couple-day span of work, I reached my limit. While cooking I grabbed the dagger from his hip and held it up against his throat. The ancient one didn’t even flinch, calmly looking me in my eyes.

annotations.comment: Yes, I wondered why he didn’t worry about missing classes or his family or his new job or his graduation…

paragraphs.body: "What's going on? When will this be over? Who are you? Why are you here alone?"
I kept spitting out questions, any and all that came to my mind. I began tearing up with frustration, not knowing how to deal with my emotions.

He slowly put 2 fingers on the blade, pushing it down, leading me to drop it in weakness, my head falling with it. He suddenly embraced me with his big open arms. The man then said softly, "Fear not, for there is always purpose."

Later that same night, I heard some impressive howling, which was a first. Certainly, some large dogs, but hopefully they weren't wild. I tried to fall asleep, and think I was able to for a short time. However, I was startled by a much closer sound of aggressive growling and yelling. I looked outside and it was the man, fighting off 3 giant wolves on his own, torch and dagger in hand. I froze, but quickly snapped out of it and grabbed a large walking stick I had (I thought it looked cool one day while cutting trees), which inherently had a roughly sharp tip from banging it on the ground all the time. I ran towards the commotion and impaled one of the unexpecting wolves from the side. This led to an opening for the man to swiftly take out the other two, as their attention sharply diverted towards me. The man was clearly not a stranger to fighting. Thinking back on it, could he have taken me out earlier in the night, even with the blade directly to his throat? Then, there was a short moment of silence as we gazed upon the lives we just took.
"Follow."
He never allowed me inside the main cabin after sundown.
"Son, you've grown."

Well yeah, I definitely got stronger with all that work, he hasn’t noticed? However, he was looking directly into my eyes. He then began to tell me his story. How he once left behind the one he loved to progress to kingship. He tried to balance love with war, quality with council, but let the duty he sought take precedent. When he came to achieve the life he spent his youth creating, he also realized what he left behind. When he tried to salvage the relations, they were far too gone. This cursed him, internally and externally, leading to his love becoming one with an ancient relic. He had not known how to reverse this curse, but figured in some way he must learn. The best way to learn, is to teach. The man then said to me:

"Every insect flies with purpose just as every tree grows with intent. The Universe makes no mistakes. You are here for a reason and have heard my story for a reason, just as I am here and just as I have told. Go on and learn from my mistakes. Love with purpose."
With slow tears dripping from both of our eyes, there was a flash, and then nothing.

When I woke up my eyes were still closed. I smelled something faintly familiar, like fresh pine and damp Earth, with a musty undertone. I felt the wet moisture start seeping into my butt. I finally opened my eyes. I was back in the woods near my home. I stood, saw the rising sun, and heard the dump truck continue up the street above.
"Wow."
I walked home without a single thought in my head, went upstairs, and laid in my bed soaked with dirt, mud, and sweat. I picked up my phone on my bedside and decided to finally text Her again.
'Hey, we should get lunch this weekend'
Typing bubbles appeared quickly, much quicker than anticipated for so early in the morning.
'I would love that'

annotations.comment: Ah, you came dangerously close to “this was all a dream”! But no, I enjoyed sections of this a lot, Natalie. You wrote your sarcastic narrator really well — I genuinely laughed out loud at times. And I love the use of the video game tropes. Your biggest issue is that what your narrator and main character wants isn’t really clear until the final few lines, when we learn, at last, that he needed courage to ask a girl out. You filled a lot of pages but you didn’t quite write that story yet. My suggestion is that you tighten your focus — cut the entire trip to the other realm and the three months of working, which, while well written, doesn’t make a ton of sense. Instead, have us go from the opening chase to the passing out and then awakening. Figure out a way to work in the women earlier so we know that the narrator is struggling to get the courage to text her, and than his experience in the woods somehow gives him that. You should end up closer to your word count, keep a lot of what works really well here, and also get us a story with a plot. Lots of fun — thank you and happy revising!
